title: Go further
text: article demonstrate random assortment content, including quite advanced. Change stat layer Change position layer Use + *_mode_* axis-lines gridlines wanted Reorder /reverse categorical variables Drop unused categorical variable values Fix either colour outline interior fill constant colour Transform scales "log" etc Change *_position positional axes Add contrasting dark light text polygons Use opacity emphasise/demphasise. Centre diverging colour scale Use replace_seq keep every nth label Understand oob & clip Use delayed evaluation Make custom gg_* wrapper functions
code: library(ggblanket) library(ggplot2) library(dplyr) library(stringr) library(tidyr) library(palmerpenguins) library(patchwork)

what: 1. Change the stat of the layer
title: Go further
text: default stat gg_* function can changed.
code: penguins |>   gg_pointrange(     stat = "summary",      x = species,     y = flipper_length_mm,      size = 0.1,   ) library(ggforce)  ggplot2::economics |>   slice_head(n = 35) |>    gg_path(     stat = "bspline", n = 100,     x = date,      y = unemploy,     linewidth = 1,   )

what: 2. Change the position of the layer
title: Go further
text: default position gg_* function can changed.
code: penguins |>    gg_point(     position = ggbeeswarm::position_quasirandom(),     x = sex,      y = flipper_length_mm,     col = sex,     mode = light_mode_n(),   )

what: 3. Use + *_mode_* where axis-lines and gridlines are not as wanted
title: Go further
text: Sometimes plot might guess removal gridlines axis-line/ticks incorrectly. situations, can + *_mode_* plot, remove whatever want.
code: msleep |>    gg_point(     x = bodywt,      y = brainwt,     col = vore,     col_labels = str_to_sentence,     x_transform = "log10",     y_transform = "log10",   ) +   light_mode_r() +   guides(     x = guide_axis_logticks(),     y = guide_axis_logticks(),   )

title: Go further
text: ggblanket requires unquoted variables x, y, col, facet, facet2 alpha. can often manipulate data prior plotting acheive want (e.g. using tidyr::drop_na, forcats::fct_rev /forcats::fct_reorder).
code: p1 <- diamonds |>   count(color) |>   gg_col(     x = n,     y = color,     width = 0.75,     x_labels = \(x) x / 1000,     x_title = "Count (thousands)",      subtitle = "\nDefault order"   )  p2 <- diamonds |>   count(color) |>   mutate(across(color, \(x) x |>                   forcats::fct_reorder(n) |>                   forcats::fct_rev())) |>   gg_col(     x = n,     y = color,     width = 0.75,     x_labels = \(x) x / 1000,     x_title = "Count (thousands)",      subtitle = "\nRe-orderered"   )  p1 + p2

title: Go further
text: ggblanket keeps unused factor levels plot. users wish drop unused levels likewise data prior plotting using forcats::fct_drop.
code: p1 <- diamonds |>    count(color) |>   filter(color %in% c("E", "G", "I")) |>   gg_col(     x = n,     y = color,     width = 0.75,     x_labels = \(x) x / 1000,     x_title = "Count (thousands)",      subtitle = "\nUnused levels kept",   )  p2 <- diamonds |>    count(color) |>   filter(color %in% c("E", "G", "I")) |>   mutate(color = forcats::fct_drop(color)) |>    gg_col(     x = n,     y = color,     width = 0.75,     x_labels = \(x) x / 1000,     x_title = "Count (thousands)",      subtitle = "\nUnused levels dropped",   )  p1 + p2

what: 6. Fix either the colour outline or interior fill to a constant colour
title: Go further
text: geom’s outline colour interior fill, possible fix one constant colour. , use colour = "black" fill = "#D3D3D3". Note requires col aesthetic work. must hacky way, want col aesthetic.
code: penguins |>   mutate(across(sex, \(x) str_to_sentence(x))) |>   gg_boxplot(     x = species,     y = flipper_length_mm,     col = sex,     colour = "black", #or fill = #D3D3D3",     position = position_dodge2(preserve = "single"),     alpha_pal = 0.9,   ) penguins |>   mutate(hack = "") |>    gg_boxplot(     x = species,     y = flipper_length_mm,     col = hack,     colour = "black", #or fill = #D3D3D3",      width = 0.5,     alpha_pal = 0.9,     mode = light_mode_n(),   )

title: Go further
text: Transform objects (e.g. scales::transform_log() character strings can used transform scales - including combining .
code:
p1 <- pressure |>   gg_point(     x = temperature,      y = pressure,      x_labels = replace_seq,     y_labels = replace_seq,     subtitle = "
Default",   )  p2 <- pressure |>   gg_point(     x = temperature,      y = pressure,      x_labels = replace_seq,     y_transform = "reverse",      y_labels = replace_seq,     subtitle = "
Reverse",   )  p3 <- pressure |>   gg_point(     x = temperature,      y = pressure,     x_labels = replace_seq,     y_transform = "log10",     subtitle = "
Log10",    )  p4 <- pressure |>   gg_point(     x = temperature,      y = pressure,      x_labels = replace_seq,     y_transform = c("log10", "reverse"),     subtitle = "
Log10 & Reverse",   )  (p1 + p2) / (p3 + p4)

title: Go further
text: Positional axes can changed using *_position. Note x_position = "top", caption must added modified make work nicely *_mode_* theme.
code: economics |>   gg_line(     x = date,     y = unemploy,     col = date,     y_position = "right",     x_position = "top",     caption = "",      title = "Unemployment",     subtitle = "1967\u20132015",   )

what: 9. Add contrasting dark or light text on polygons
title: Go further
text: Text polygons can coloured dark light colour determined based underlying polygon colour using !!!aes_contrast(). method developed Teun van den Brand (@teunbrand). lightness, greyness darkness colour vectors work nicely applicable *_mode_* theme.
code: penguins |>   count(species, sex) |>   gg_col(     position = position_dodge2(preserve = "single"),     x = sex,     y = n,     col = species,     width = 0.75,     x_labels = \(x) str_to_sentence(x),   ) +   geom_text(     mapping = aes(label = n, !!!aes_contrast(lightness)),     position = position_dodge2(width = 0.75, preserve = "single"),     vjust = 1.33,     show.legend = FALSE,   ) penguins |>   count(species, sex) |>   gg_col(     position = position_dodge2(preserve = "single"),     x = n,     y = sex,     col = species,     width = 0.75,     y_labels = \(x) str_to_sentence(x),     mode = dark_mode_r(),   ) +   geom_text(     mapping = aes(label = n, !!!aes_contrast(darkness)),     position = position_dodge2(width = 0.75, preserve = "single"),     hjust = 1.25,     show.legend = FALSE,   )

title: Go further
text: Use opacity alpha, col, alpha_pal/col_pal emphasise/demphasise parts visualisation.
code:
p1 <- penguins |>    drop_na(sex) |>    mutate(across(sex, str_to_sentence)) |>    gg_density(     x = flipper_length_mm,     x_breaks = scales::breaks_pretty(n = 3),     y_labels = replace_seq,     col_pal = scales::alpha(orange, 0.33),     alpha_pal = 0.1,     subtitle = "
Opacity fill 0.1 & outline 0.33"   )  p2 <- penguins |>    drop_na(sex) |>    mutate(across(sex, str_to_sentence)) |>    gg_density(     x = flipper_length_mm,     col = sex,     x_breaks = scales::breaks_pretty(n = 3),     y_labels = replace_seq,     col_pal = c(orange, "#78909C"),     subtitle = "
col variable"   )  p3 <- penguins |>    drop_na(sex) |>    mutate(across(sex, str_to_sentence)) |>    gg_density(     x = flipper_length_mm,     col = sex,      alpha = sex,     x_breaks = scales::breaks_pretty(n = 3),     y_labels = replace_seq,     col_pal = c(orange, "#78909C"),     alpha_pal = c(0.5, 0.1),     subtitle = "
col and alpha variable",   ) +   guides(colour = "none", fill = "none", alpha = "none")  p4 <- penguins |>    drop_na(sex) |>    mutate(across(sex, str_to_sentence)) |>    gg_density(     x = flipper_length_mm,     col = sex,      alpha = sex,     x_breaks = scales::breaks_pretty(n = 3),     y_labels = replace_seq,     col_pal = c(orange, scales::alpha("#78909C", 0.25)),     alpha_pal = c(0.5, 0.1),     subtitle = "
col and alpha variable & outline opacity",   )  (p1 + p2) / (p3 + p4)

title: Go further
text: Sometimes need centre diverging colour scale.
code: mpg |>   mutate(centred = cty - mean(cty)) |>   select(displ, hwy, centred) %>%   gg_point(     x = displ,     y = hwy,     col = centred,     col_pal = c(teal, greys[1], orange),     col_breaks = scales::breaks_width(5),     col_rescale = scales::rescale(c(min(.$centred), 0, max(.$centred)))   ) mpg |>   mutate(centred = cty - mean(cty)) |>   select(displ, hwy, centred) %>%   gg_point(     x = displ,     y = hwy,     col = centred,     col_pal = c(teal, greys[1], orange),     col_limits = max(abs(.$centred)) * c(-1, 1),     col_breaks = scales::breaks_width(5)   )

what: 12. Use replace_seq to keep every nth label
title: Go further
text: replace_seq helper function provided replace sequence labels "".
code: penguins |>    gg_point(     x = flipper_length_mm,     y = body_mass_g,     col = bill_length_mm,     x_labels = replace_seq,     y_labels = replace_seq,     col_steps = TRUE,     col_breaks = scales::breaks_pretty(5),     col_labels = replace_seq,   ) penguins |>   count(species, sex) |>   gg_col(     position = position_dodge2(preserve = "single"),     x = n,     y = sex,     col = species,     width = 0.75,     y_labels = \(x) str_to_sentence(x),     mode = dark_mode_r(),   ) +   geom_text(     mapping = aes(label = n, !!!aes_contrast(darkness)),     position = position_dodge2(width = 0.75, preserve = "single"),     hjust = 1.25,     show.legend = FALSE,   )

title: Go further
text: default, ggblanket keeps values outside limits (*_oob = scales::oob_keep) calculating geoms scales plot. also clip anything outside cartesian coordinate space default (coord = ggplot2::coord_cartesian(clip = "")). ggplot2 default drops values outside limits calculating geoms scales plot (scales::oob_censor), clips anything outside cartesian coordinate space (coord = ggplot2::coord_cartesian(clip = "")). needs considered particularly using x_limits y_limits, stat "identity".
code:
p1 <- economics |>    gg_smooth(     x = date,      y = unemploy,      x_labels = \(x) stringr::str_sub(x, 3, 4),     subtitle = "
No x_limits set",      se = TRUE) +   geom_vline(xintercept = c(lubridate::ymd("1985-01-01", "1995-01-01")),              col = blue,               linetype = 3) +   geom_point(col = blue, alpha = 0.3)  p2 <- economics |>    filter(between(date, lubridate::ymd("1985-01-01"), lubridate::ymd("1995-01-01"))) |>    gg_smooth(     x = date,      y = unemploy,     se = TRUE,     x_labels = \(x) stringr::str_sub(x, 3, 4),     subtitle = "
x data filtered") +   geom_point(col = blue, alpha = 0.3)  p1 + p2 p3 <- economics |>    gg_smooth(     x = date,      y = unemploy,      se = TRUE,     x_limits = c(lubridate::ymd("1985-01-01", "1995-01-01")),     x_labels = \(x) stringr::str_sub(x, 3, 4),     subtitle = "
x_limits set (with default oob_keep)",    ) +   geom_point(col = blue, alpha = 0.3)  p3 + plot_spacer() p4 <- economics |>    gg_smooth(     coord = coord_cartesian(clip = "on"),      x = date,      y = unemploy,      se = TRUE,     x_limits = c(lubridate::ymd("1985-01-01", "1995-01-01")),     x_labels = \(x) stringr::str_sub(x, 3, 4),     subtitle = "
x_limits set  (with default oob_keep)
& cartesian space clipped") +   geom_point(col = blue, alpha = 0.3)  p5 <- economics |>    gg_smooth(     x = date,      y = unemploy,      se = TRUE,     x_limits = c(lubridate::ymd("1985-01-01", "1995-01-01")),     x_labels = \(x) stringr::str_sub(x, 3, 4),     x_oob = scales::oob_censor,     subtitle = "
x_limits set & oob_censor") +   geom_point(col = blue, alpha = 0.3)  p4 + p5

title: Go further
text: x, y, col alpha aesthetic arguments require unquoted variable, apply function . However, can aesthetics within mapping argument. can used delayed evaluation ggplot2::after_stat function.
code: penguins |>   gg_histogram(     x = flipper_length_mm,     mapping = aes(y = after_stat(density)),     facet = species,   ) faithfuld |>   gg_contour(     x = waiting,     y = eruptions,     z = density,     mapping = aes(colour = after_stat(level)),     bins = 8,   )

title: Go further
text: may wish make wrapper functions continually adding defaults gg_* functions. essentially allows choose defaults functions. Use ... argument access arguments gg_* function. alternative method enquo !! use {{ }}.
code: gg_point_custom <- function(data,                              x = NULL,                              y = NULL,                              col = NULL,                             col_pal = NULL,                             shape = 17,                             size = 3,                             titles_to_case = snakecase::to_title_case,                             ...) {      x <- enquo(x)   y <- enquo(y)   col <- enquo(col)      if (rlang::is_null(col_pal)) {     if (rlang::quo_is_null(col)) col_pal <- plum     else col_pal <- RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(n = 8, "Dark2")   }      data |>      gg_point(       x = !!x,       y = !!y,       col = !!col,       col_pal = col_pal,       shape = shape,       size = size,        titles_to_case = titles_to_case,       ...,     )  }  penguins |>    gg_point_custom(     x = flipper_length_mm,     y = body_mass_g,   ) penguins |>    gg_point_custom(     x = flipper_length_mm,     y = body_mass_g,     col = species,   )

title: Extensions
text: article demonstrate fantastic ggplot2 extension packages.
code: library(ggblanket) library(ggplot2) library(dplyr) library(stringr) library(palmerpenguins)

title: Extensions
text: patchwork package enables plots patched together. convenient hack add panel centred title can add facet_wrap layer character title string facets argument. Otherwise need modify margins
code: library(patchwork)  p1 <- mtcars |>    gg_point(     x = mpg,      y = disp,     y_labels = replace_seq,   ) +   facet_wrap(~"Plot 1")  p2 <- mtcars |>    gg_boxplot(     x = gear,      y = disp,      group = gear,      width = 0.5,     y_labels = replace_seq,   ) +   facet_wrap(~"Plot 2")  p3 <- mtcars |>    gg_smooth(     x = disp,      y = qsec,      x_breaks = scales::breaks_pretty(4),    ) +   facet_wrap(~"Plot 3")   (p1 + p2) / p3 +   plot_annotation(     title = "A collection of plots patched together",     subtitle = "Works so nice!",     theme = light_mode_r() +       theme(         plot.title = element_text(margin = margin(t = -11)),         plot.subtitle = element_text(margin = margin(t = 5.5)),       )   )

title: Extensions
text: ggtext package support using markdown text. mdthemes package makes easy convert ggplot2::element_text ggtext::element_markdown themes.
code: penguins |> gg_point( x = bill_depth_mm, y = bill_length_mm, col = species, facet = island, facet2 = sex, x_labels = \(x) glue::glue("_{x}_"), x_title = "**Bill depth** (mm)", y_labels = \(x) replace_seq(glue::glue("_{x}_")), y_title = "**Bill length** (mm)", facet_labels = \(x) glue::glue("_{x}_"), title = "***Pygoscelis*** *penguin* bill **lengths** and depths", subtitle = "**Adelie**, **Chinstrap**, *and* **Gentoo**", mode = mdthemes::as_md_theme(light_mode_n()) )

title: Extensions
text: showtext package enables use different fonts plots. *_mode_* theme functions provide base_family argument.
code: head(sysfonts::font_families_google()) #> [1] "ABeeZee"       "Abel"          "Abhaya Libre"  "Abril Fatface" #> [5] "Aclonica"      "Acme"  sysfonts::font_add_google("Covered By Your Grace", "grace") sysfonts::font_add_google('Roboto Slab', 'roboto_slab') sysfonts::font_add_google('Syne Mono', 'syne')  showtext::showtext_auto(enable = TRUE)  penguins |>   mutate(across(sex, \(x) str_to_sentence(x))) |>   gg_point(     x = flipper_length_mm,     y = body_mass_g,     col = sex,     facet = species,     title = "Penguins body mass by flipper length",     subtitle = "Palmer Archipelago, Antarctica",     caption = "Source: Gorman, 2020",     mode = light_mode_r(base_family = "grace")   ) +   theme(     plot.title = element_text(family = "roboto_slab"),     plot.subtitle = element_text(family = "syne")   ) showtext::showtext_auto(enable = FALSE)

title: Extensions
text: ggforce package includes numerous extra geoms, stats etc.
code: library(ggforce)  economics |>   slice_head(n = 35) |>    gg_path(     stat = "bspline",     x = date,      y = unemploy,     n = 100,      linewidth = 1,   ) penguins |>   gg_blanket(     geom = "mark_hull",     x = flipper_length_mm,     y = body_mass_g,     col = species,     coord = coord_cartesian(),     alpha_pal = 0.5,   ) +   geom_point() iris |>    mutate(across(Species, \(x) str_to_sentence(x))) |>    gg_blanket(     geom = "shape",     stat = "voronoi_tile",     x = Sepal.Length,      y = Sepal.Width,      col = Species,     group = 1,     colour = "#fcfdfe",   )

title: Extensions
text: ggbeeswarm package enables beeswarm plots.
code: p1 <- penguins |>    gg_point(     position = ggbeeswarm::position_beeswarm(),     x = sex,      y = flipper_length_mm,     col = sex,     x_labels = \(x) str_to_upper(str_sub(x, 1, 1)),     subtitle = "\nBeeswarm",     mode = light_mode_n(),   )   p2 <- penguins |>    gg_point(     position = ggbeeswarm::position_quasirandom(),     x = sex,      y = flipper_length_mm,     col = sex,     x_labels = \(x) str_to_upper(str_sub(x, 1, 1)),     subtitle = "\nQuasirandom",     mode = light_mode_n(),   )   p1 + p2

title: Extensions
text: ggrepel package can used neatly avoid overlapping labels.
code: mtcars |>   tibble::rownames_to_column("car") |>   filter(wt > 2.75, wt < 3.45) |>   gg_point(     x = wt,      y = mpg,      label = car,    ) +   ggrepel::geom_text_repel(colour = blue)

title: Extensions
text: ggridges package enables ridgeline plots.
code: library(ggridges)  ggridges::Catalan_elections |>   rename_with(snakecase::to_snake_case) |>   mutate(year = factor(year)) |>   gg_blanket(     geom = "density_ridges",     stat = "density_ridges",     coord = coord_cartesian(),     x = percent,     y = year,     col = option,     colour = "white",     x_limits = c(0, 100),     y_expand = expansion(c(0, 0.125)),     alpha_pal = 0.7,   )

title: Extensions
text: ggdist package enables visualisation uncertainty.
code: library(ggdist) library(distributional) set.seed(123)  data.frame(   distribution = c("Gamma(2,1)", "Normal(5,1)", "Mixture"),   values = c(     dist_gamma(2, 1),     dist_normal(5, 1),     dist_mixture(       dist_gamma(2, 1),       dist_normal(5, 1),       weights = c(0.4, 0.6))   )) |>    gg_blanket(     geom = "slabinterval",     stat = "slabinterval",     y = distribution,     mapping = aes(dist = values),     y_expand = c(0.05, 0.05),     alpha_pal = 0.5,     mode = light_mode_n(),     # show_slab = FALSE,     # show_interval = FALSE,     # side = "both",   ) set.seed(123)  tibble(x = 1:10) |>    group_by(across(everything()))  |>    do(tibble(y = rnorm(100, .$x))) |>    median_qi(.width = c(0.5, 0.8, 0.95)) |>   mutate(.width = factor(.width)) |>   gg_blanket(     geom = "lineribbon",     x = x,     y = y,     ymin = .lower,     ymax = .upper,     col = .width,     colour = lightness[1],     col_legend_rev = TRUE,     col_pal = rev(RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(n = 3, "Blues")),   )

title: Extensions
text: ggdensity package provides visualizations density estimates.
code: library(ggdensity) set.seed(123)  data.frame(   x = rnorm(1000),   y = rnorm(1000),   z = c(rep("A", times = 500), rep("B", times = 500)) ) |>   gg_blanket(     geom = "hdr",      stat = "hdr",     x = x,      y = y,      colour = NA,     y_limits = c(NA, NA),     col_pal = alpha(blue, 0),   ) set.seed(123)  data.frame(   x = rnorm(1000),   y = rnorm(1000),   z = c(rep("A", times = 500), rep("B", times = 500)) ) |>   gg_blanket(     geom = "hdr",      stat = "hdr",     x = x,      y = y,      col = z,     facet = z,     y_limits = c(NA, NA),     colour = NA,   )

title: Extensions
text: geomtextpath package enables curved text.
code: library(geomtextpath)  expand.grid(x = 1:nrow(volcano), y = 1:ncol(volcano)) |>    mutate(z = c(volcano)) |>    gg_contour_filled(     x = x,      y = y,     z = z,     binwidth = 20,     x_expand = c(0, 0),     x_limits = c(NA, NA),     y_limits = c(NA, NA)) +   geom_textcontour(     aes(label = after_stat(level)),      size = 2.5,      straight = TRUE,   ) +   ggeasy::easy_remove_x_axis() +    ggeasy::easy_remove_y_axis()

title: Extensions
text: ggblend package provides blending colours. Note must use graphics device supports blending.
code: p1 <- penguins |>   gg_density(     x = flipper_length_mm,     col = species,     y_limits = c(0, NA),     mode = light_mode_n(),   ) +   facet_wrap(~"Normal") +   ggeasy::easy_remove_x_axis() +   ggeasy::easy_remove_y_axis()  p2 <- penguins |>   gg_blanket(     stat = "density",     x = flipper_length_mm,     col = species,     y_limits = c(0, NA),     mode = light_mode_n(),   ) +   facet_wrap(~"Blended") +   geom_density(alpha = 0.5) |> ggblend::blend(blend = "multiply") +   ggeasy::easy_remove_x_axis() +   ggeasy::easy_remove_y_axis()  p1 + p2

title: Extensions
text: ggh4x package includes enhanced facet functionality.
code: iris |>    rename_with(\(x) snakecase::to_snake_case(x)) |>    mutate(across(species, \(x) str_to_sentence(x))) |>    mutate(size = if_else(species == "Setosa", "Short Leaves", "Long Leaves")) |>    gg_point(     x = sepal_width,      y = sepal_length,      x_breaks = scales::breaks_pretty(n = 3),   ) +   ggh4x::facet_nested(     cols = vars(size, species),      nest_line = TRUE,      solo_line = TRUE,   ) +   theme(strip.text.x = element_text(margin = margin(t = 2.5, b = 5)))

title: Extensions
text: gghighlight package enables geoms parts thereof highlighted.  gg_* function builds scale data thinks within panel. Therefore situations, may need make adjustments, scale builds correctly.
code: penguins |>   gg_point(     x = flipper_length_mm,     y = body_mass_g,   ) +   gghighlight::gghighlight(body_mass_g >= 5000) penguins |>   gg_histogram(     x = flipper_length_mm,     col = species,     x_breaks = scales::breaks_pretty(4),     col_pal = rep(blue, 3),    ) + #build the scale for not faceted (i.e. all data)   facet_wrap(~species) + #then add facet layer   gghighlight::gghighlight()

title: Extensions
text: ggeasy package provides lot support easily modifying themes. Removing axes gridlines often useful.
code: library(ggeasy)  penguins |>   mutate(across(sex, str_to_sentence)) |>    gg_jitter(     x = species,      y = body_mass_g,      col = sex,    ) +   light_mode_t() +   easy_remove_y_gridlines() +   easy_remove_x_axis(what = c("line", "ticks")) +   easy_remove_legend_title()

title: Extensions
text: ggnewscale package enables multiple colour scales.
code: penguins |>    gg_blanket(     x = flipper_length_mm,      y = body_mass_g,   ) +    geom_point(     mapping = aes(colour = bill_length_mm),      data = penguins |> filter(species == "Gentoo"),   ) +   scale_color_viridis_c(option = "G", direction = -1) +   labs(colour = "Gentoo\nBill length (mm)") +   ggnewscale::new_scale_colour() +   geom_point(     mapping = aes(x = flipper_length_mm, y = body_mass_g, colour = species),      data = penguins |> filter(species != "Gentoo"),   ) +   scale_color_manual(values = c(plum, orange)) +   labs(colour = "Species")

title: Extensions
text: ggiraph package enables interactive plots.
code: p <- diamonds |>   gg_blanket(     x = color,     y_expand_limits = 0,     stat = "count",      mode = light_mode_r(base_size = 9, base_family = "arial"),   ) +   ggiraph::geom_bar_interactive(     mapping = aes(tooltip = after_stat(count), data_id = color),      width = 0.75,      colour = blue,      fill = blue,      alpha = 0.9   )  ggiraph::girafe(   ggobj = p,    height_svg = 3,    width_svg = 5,   options = list(     ggiraph::opts_sizing(rescale = TRUE, width = 0.7),     ggiraph::opts_tooltip(use_fill = TRUE),      ggiraph::opts_hover(css = "stroke:#121B24;stroke-width:1px;"))   )

title: Extensions
text: plotly::ggplotly function enables interactive plots. Using text aesthetic provides flexibility tooltips. Alternatively, can use plotly::ggplotly(p, tooltip = c("x", "y", "fill")) plotly::ggplotly(p) quick tooltips.
code: p <- diamonds |>   group_by(color) |>   count(name = "count") |>   mutate(text = glue::glue("Colour: {color}                             Count: {count}")) |>   gg_col(     x = color,     y = count,     text = text,     width = 0.75,     y_expand_limits = 0,     mode = light_mode_r(base_family = "arial"),   )  plotly::ggplotly(p, tooltip = "text")

title: Demo: light mode
text: article demonstrate wrapper function.
code: library(ggblanket) library(ggplot2) library(dplyr) library(stringr) library(tidyr) library(palmerpenguins)

title: Demo: light mode
code: economics |>   gg_area(     x = date,     y = unemploy,     y_title = "Unemployment",   )

title: Demo: light mode
code: penguins |>   mutate(across(sex, str_to_sentence)) |>    gg_bar(     position = position_dodge(preserve = "single"),     y = species,     col = sex,     width = 0.75,   )

title: Demo: light mode
code: diamonds |>   gg_bin_2d(     x = carat,     y = price,   )

title: Demo: light mode
code: penguins |>   drop_na(sex) |>    mutate(across(sex, str_to_sentence)) |>    gg_boxplot(     x = flipper_length_mm,     y = sex,     col = species,     mode = light_mode_b(),   )

title: Demo: light mode
code: penguins |>   drop_na(sex) |>    mutate(across(sex, str_to_sentence)) |>    group_by(sex, species) |>   summarise(across(flipper_length_mm, \(x) mean(x, na.rm = TRUE))) |>   gg_col(     position = position_dodge(preserve = "single"),     x = flipper_length_mm,     y = species,     col = sex,     width = 0.75,   )

title: Demo: light mode
code: faithfuld |>  gg_contour(    x = waiting,    y = eruptions,    z = density,    )

title: Demo: light mode
code: faithfuld |>  gg_contour_filled(    x = waiting,    y = eruptions,    z = density,    bins = 8,   )

title: Demo: light mode
code: data.frame(   trt = factor(c(1, 1, 2, 2)),   resp = c(1, 5, 3, 4),   group = factor(c(1, 2, 1, 2)),   upper = c(1.1, 5.3, 3.3, 4.2),   lower = c(0.8, 4.6, 2.4, 3.6)) |>   gg_crossbar(     x = trt,     y = resp,     ymin = lower,     ymax = upper,     col = group,     width = 0.5,     x_title = "Treatment",     y_title = "Response",   )

title: Demo: light mode

title: Demo: light mode
code: data.frame(   trt = factor(c(1, 1, 2, 2)),   resp = c(1, 5, 3, 4),   group = factor(c(1, 2, 1, 2)),   upper = c(1.1, 5.3, 3.3, 4.2),   lower = c(0.8, 4.6, 2.4, 3.6)   ) |>   gg_errorbar(     x = trt,     ymin = lower,     ymax = upper,     col = group,     width = 0.1,     x_title = "Treatment",     y_title = "Response",  )

code: penguins |>   mutate(across(sex, str_to_sentence)) |>    gg_freqpoly(     x = flipper_length_mm,     col = sex,     mode = light_mode_t(),   ) +   labs(colour = NULL, fill = NULL)

title: ggblanket
text: ggblanket package ggplot2 wrapper functions. primary objective simplify ggplot2 visualisation. Secondary objectives relate : Design: produce well-designed visualisation Alignment: use conventions aligned ggplot2 Scope: cover much ggplot2 .

title: ggblanket
text: Almost forty gg_* wrapper functions col argument colour fill variable facet argument facet variable facet2 argument facet 2nd variable col_pal argument customise colours Numerous prefixed arguments customise Unspecified titles converted snakecase::sentence_case mode argument designed provided *_mode_* themes Families *_mode_* themes differ legend placement symmetrical continuous scale easy opt-Access geom_* arguments via ... Turn-either coloured outlines interiors Ability add multiple geom_* layers Use alpha aesthetics gg_blanket() function geom flexibility
code: library(ggblanket) library(ggplot2) library(dplyr) library(stringr) library(tidyr) library(palmerpenguins) library(patchwork)

title: ggblanket
text: gg_* function wraps ggplot2 ggplot2::ggplot() function applicable ggplot2 geom_*() function. gg_* function named geom_* function wrap.
code: penguins |>   gg_point(     x = flipper_length_mm,     y = body_mass_g,   )

what: 2. A single col argument to colour and fill by a variable
title: ggblanket
text: colour fill aesthetics ggplot2 merged single concept represented col argument. combined aesthetic means everything coloured according , .e. coloured outlines filled interiors.
code: penguins |>   gg_point(     x = flipper_length_mm,     y = body_mass_g,      col = species,   ) penguins |>   drop_na(sex) |>   mutate(across(sex, str_to_sentence)) |>   gg_bar(     position = "dodge",     y = species,      col = sex,     width = 0.75,   )

what: 3. A facet argument to facet by a variable
title: ggblanket
text: Faceting treated aesthetic. Users just provide unquoted variable facet . Whenever facet variable provided (facet2 variable provided), facet_layout default "wrap".
code: penguins |>   drop_na(sex) |>    mutate(across(sex, str_to_sentence)) |>    gg_histogram(     x = flipper_length_mm,     facet = species,   )

what: 4. A facet2 argument to facet by a 2nd variable
title: ggblanket
text: facet2 argument also provided extra functionality flexibility. enables users facet easily "grid" layout facet variable horizontally facet2 variable vertically. Whenever facet2 variable provided, facet_layout default "grid".
code: penguins |>   mutate(across(sex, str_to_sentence)) |>    gg_histogram(     x = flipper_length_mm,     facet = species,     facet2 = sex,   )

what: 5. A col_pal argument to customise colours
title: ggblanket
text: col_pal argument used customise colours geom. user can provide vector colours argument. can named . works consistent way - regardless whether col aesthetic added .
code: penguins |>   gg_jitter(     x = species,      y = body_mass_g,      col_pal = "#7FCDBB",   ) viridisLite::rocket(n = 9) #> [1] "#03051AFF" "#30173AFF" "#611F53FF" "#961C5BFF" "#CB1B4FFF" "#EC4B3EFF" #> [7] "#F4875EFF" "#F6BB97FF" "#FAEBDDFF"  sf::st_read(system.file("shape/nc.shp", package = "sf"), quiet = TRUE) |>    gg_sf(     col = AREA,     col_pal = viridisLite::rocket(n = 9)   )

what: 6. Numerous other prefixed arguments to customise
title: ggblanket
text: ggblanket offers numerous arguments customise prefixed whether relate x, y, col, facet alpha. prefixed arguments work nicely Rstudio autocomplete, users ensure settings support use tab autocompletions multi-line autocompletions (.e. Tools - Global Options - Code - Completion).
code: penguins |>   drop_na(sex) |>     gg_jitter(     x = species,     y = body_mass_g,     col = flipper_length_mm,     facet = sex,     x_labels = \(x) str_sub(x, 1, 1),     y_breaks = scales::breaks_width(1000),     y_expand_limits = 2000,     y_labels = scales::label_number(big.mark = " "),      y_transform = "log10",     y_title = "Body mass (g)",     col_steps = TRUE,     col_breaks = \(x) quantile(x, seq(0, 1, 0.25)),     facet_labels = str_to_sentence,   )

title: ggblanket
text: Unspecified x, y, col alpha titles converted sentence case snakecase::to_sentence. However, title can manually changed using applicable *_title argument. default snakecase::to_sentence conversion can turned using titles_to_case = \(x) x changed (e.g. snakecase::to_title_case).
code: diamonds |>   gg_hex(     coord = coord_cartesian(clip = "on"),      x = carat,     y = price,     y_limits = c(0, 20000),   )

what: 8. A mode argument designed for provided *_mode_* themes
title: ggblanket
text: mode argument designed use *_mode_* themes. mode provided , gg_* function : guess whether orientation plot vertical graphs, remove x grid-lines, x axis-line/ticks horizontal graphs, remove y grid-lines, y axis-line/ticks avoid side-effects, + theme output gg_*. mode_set sets theme globally side-effects - ggplot2::theme_set sets globally without side-effects. Use mode_set(light_mode_r()) theme_set(theme_grey()) unset. Note actually set theme globally theme_grey(11) reason - instead use theme_grey(11.01).

what: 9. Families of *_mode_* themes that differ by legend placement
title: ggblanket
text: Three theme families provided: light_mode_*, grey_mode_* dark_mode_*. theme family provides 4 variants differ based legend placement, represented suffix mode name r (right), b (bottom), t (top) n (none). default light_mode_r. intended use mode argument. want use quick non-bold title, use subtitle = "\n...". title can removed using + labs(... = NULL). removing legend associated col, add colour = NULL fill = NULL. alternative approach add + theme(legend.title = element_blank()) plot.
code: penguins |>   gg_histogram(     x = flipper_length_mm,     col = species,     title = "Penguin flipper length by species",     subtitle = "Palmer Archipelago, Antarctica",     caption = "Source: Gorman, 2020",      mode = light_mode_t(),   ) +   labs(colour = NULL, fill = NULL) penguins |>   gg_histogram(     x = flipper_length_mm,     col = species,     title = "Penguin flipper length by species",     subtitle = "Palmer Archipelago, Antarctica",     caption = "Source: Gorman, 2020",      mode = grey_mode_b(),   ) penguins |>   gg_histogram(     x = flipper_length_mm,     col = species,     col_pal = c(teal, orange, plum),     title = "Penguin flipper length by species",     subtitle = "Palmer Archipelago, Antarctica",     caption = "Source: Gorman, 2020",      mode = dark_mode_r(),   )

what: 10. A symmetrical continuous scale with easy opt-out
title: ggblanket
text: flipped = FALSE graphs, gg_* function create symmetric continuous y scale default : y_limits range y_breaks y_expand c(0, 0) flipped = TRUE, vice-versa occurs. Sometimes may need add *_expand_limits = 0, lower bound symmetric scale falls number close 0. symmetric scale can turned easily using *_limits = c(NA, NA) (*_limits = c(0, NA) bars etc). Note symmetric scale occur scale transformation "identity", "reverse", "date", "time" "hms" - positional scale binned.

what: 11. Access to other geom_* arguments via ...
title: ggblanket
text: ... argument provides access arguments geom_*() function.
code: penguins |>   mutate(across(sex, str_to_sentence)) |>    drop_na(sex) |>    gg_smooth(     x = flipper_length_mm,     y = body_mass_g,     col = sex,      se = TRUE, # via ... from geom_smooth     level = 0.999, # via ... from geom_smooth   )

what: 12. Turn-off either coloured outlines or filled interiors
title: ggblanket
text: geom’s coloured outlines interiors, coloured default. can turn one alpha_pal = 0 filled interior, col_pal = scales::alpha(..., alpha = 0) coloured outlines. coloured outlines col argument specified, might need also modify col_pal_na value using col_pal_na = scales::alpha("darkgrey", alpha = 0). [Note col argument specified, another method use fill = NA/colour = NA.]
code: penguins |>   gg_boxplot(     position = position_dodge2(preserve = "single"),     x = flipper_length_mm,     y = species,     alpha_pal = 0, #or col_pal = scales::alpha(blue, 0),   ) penguins |>   gg_boxplot(     position = position_dodge2(preserve = "single"),     x = species,     y = flipper_length_mm,     col = sex,     alpha_pal = 0,   )

what: 13. Ability to add multiple geom_* layers
title: ggblanket
text: Users can make plots multiple layers ggblanket adding ggplot2::geom_* layers. gg_* function puts aesthetic variables (.e. x, y, col) within wrapped ggplot function. Therefore, aesthetics inherit subsequent layers added - unless inherit.aes = FALSE argument added. gg_*() function appropriate bottom layer plot, geoms drawn order. aesthetics required plot whole generally added gg_* function, including col argument. want first layer colour fill aesthetic, wish later layers access - use gg_blanket() (defaults blank geom).
code: penguins |>   group_by(species) |>   summarise(body_mass_g = mean(body_mass_g, na.rm = TRUE)) |>   mutate(lower = body_mass_g * 0.95) |>    mutate(upper = body_mass_g * 1.2) %>%   gg_col(     x = body_mass_g,     xmin = lower,      xmax = upper,     y = species,     col = species,     width = 0.75,     x_expand_limits = c(0, max(.$upper)),     x_labels = \(x) x / 1000,      x_title = "Body mass kg",    ) +   geom_errorbar(     colour = "black",      width = 0.1,    ) penguins |>   group_by(species) |>   summarise(body_mass_g = mean(body_mass_g, na.rm = TRUE)) |>   mutate(lower = body_mass_g * 0.95) |>    mutate(upper = body_mass_g * 1.2) |>    gg_blanket(      x = body_mass_g,     y = species,     col = species,     xmin = lower,      xmax = upper,     width = 0.75,     x_expand_limits = 0,     x_labels = \(x) x / 1000,      x_title = "Body mass kg",   ) +   geom_col(     colour = "#d3d3d3",     fill = "#d3d3d3",     alpha = 0.9,     width = 0.75,   ) +   geom_errorbar(     width = 0.1,    )

title: ggblanket
text: alpha fully supported ggblanket similar way col. alpha argument represents aesthetic, whereas alpha_pal represents value (values range). mapping argument provides access aesthetics, size, shape, linetype linewidth etc. Legend titles element order must aligned, legends merge. Subsequently, may need change title using + ggplot2::labs - even reverse guide.
code: penguins |>    gg_point(     x = flipper_length_mm,      y = body_mass_g,      col = species,     alpha = species,     alpha_pal = c(1, 1, 0.33),     mapping = aes(shape = species),   )

what: 15. A gg_blanket function with geom flexibility
title: ggblanket
text: ggblanket driven gg_blanket function, geom argument geom_blank defaults. functions wrap function locked-geom, default stat position arguments per applicable geom_* function.
code: penguins |>   gg_blanket(     geom = "bar",     stat = "bin",     position = "stack",     x = flipper_length_mm,      col = species,   ) penguins |>   drop_na(sex) |>   mutate(across(sex, str_to_sentence)) |>   gg_blanket(     geom = "violin",     stat = "ydensity",     position = "dodge",     x = sex,     y = body_mass_g,     col = species,   )

title: Polygon ggplot — gg_polygon
code: library(ggplot2) library(dplyr)  ids <- factor(c("1.1", "2.1", "1.2", "2.2", "1.3", "2.3"))  values <- data.frame(   id = ids,   value = c(3, 3.1, 3.1, 3.2, 3.15, 3.5) )  positions <- data.frame(   id = rep(ids, each = 4),   x = c(2, 1, 1.1, 2.2, 1, 0, 0.3, 1.1, 2.2, 1.1, 1.2, 2.5, 1.1, 0.3,         0.5, 1.2, 2.5, 1.2, 1.3, 2.7, 1.2, 0.5, 0.6, 1.3),   y = c(-0.5, 0, 1, 0.5, 0, 0.5, 1.5, 1, 0.5, 1, 2.1, 1.7, 1, 1.5,         2.2, 2.1, 1.7, 2.1, 3.2, 2.8, 2.1, 2.2, 3.3, 3.2) )  datapoly <- merge(values, positions, by = c("id"))  datapoly |>   gg_polygon(     x = x,     y = y,     col = value,     group = id,   ) #> Warning: Ignoring unknown parameters: `contour` #> Warning: Ignoring unknown parameters: `contour`

title: Rect ggplot — gg_rect
code: library(ggplot2) library(dplyr)  data.frame(   x = rep(c(2, 5, 7, 9, 12), 2),   y = rep(c(1, 2), each = 5),   z = factor(c(rep(1:3, each = 3), 4)),   w = rep(diff(c(0, 4, 6, 8, 10, 14)), 2)) |>   mutate(     xmin = x - w / 2,     xmax = x + w / 2,     ymin = y,     ymax = y + 1   ) |>   gg_rect(     xmin = xmin,     xmax = xmax,     ymin = ymin,     ymax = ymax,     col = z,   ) #> Warning: Ignoring unknown parameters: `contour` #> Warning: Ignoring unknown parameters: `contour`

title: Set the mode — mode_set
text: Set theme mode argument gg_* function NULL. Use mode_set(light_mode_r()) unset set mode. Note ggplot2::theme_set() sets globally new theme added gg_* function output side-effects. Use ggplot2::theme_set(theme_grey()) unset set theme.

title: ggblanket 6.0.0
text: Rewrote code completely. Exported gg_blanket function, allows ggproto geom (character string) added. Added aes_contrast colour aesthetic automatically contrasts fill aesthetic based work (@teunbrand, #649) ** Added alpha aesthetic support new alpha, alpha_pal alpha_* arguments. Added stat support ggproto object (character string). Added position support ggproto object (character string). Added transform support transform class object (character string). Added facet_axes facet_axis_labels arguments. Added facet_labels_position argument. Added gg_quantile, gg_rug, gg_function functions. Added col_steps argument support colouring steps. Added *_position argument support changing axis positions. Added mode_set function. Breaking: made theme_set set theme globally side-effects. Breaking: Added light_mode_*, dark_mode_* grey_mode_* family functions. Updated gg_* functions pretty removal axis line ticks. Changed default theme place legend top right. Changed NULL effect pretty axis *_limits = c(NA, NA) y_expand NULL. Improved default gridlines, changed default colour. Fixed bug col logical class. Added new helper hex codes blue, teal, orange, navy, plum, greys Updated default discrete palette. Breaking: shift ... front require users name arguments. Breaking: renamed theme argument mode. Breaking: renamed pal col_pal. Breaking: renamed pal_na col_pal_na. Breaking: renamed alpha alpha_pal. Breaking: alpha now refers aesthetic . Breaking: renamed *_include *_expand_limits. Breaking: renamed *_trans *_transform. Breaking: removed col_legend_place argument. Breaking: removed *_sec_axis argument. Breaking: removed light_mode dark_mode functions. Breaking: removed col_scale argument. Breaking: removed gg_blank function. Breaking: removed *_gridlines arguments. Breaking: renamed facet_switch argument facet_labels_switch. Breaking: removed guardian function. Breaking: renamed str_keep_seq replace_seq. Breaking: renamed titles titles_to_case. Breaking: removed magic *_title = "" removed title. Improved log transform defaults. Changed legend.byrow FALSE. gg_* function’s help now inherits parameters gg_blanket (@olivroy, #625). improved documentation help (@olivroy, #643).

title: ggblanket 5.2.0
text: CRAN release: 2023-11-10 Updated colours: #357BA2 mako[9](5) col aesthetic, guardian discrete 4 less colours, scales:hue_pal 5 colours, viridisLite::mako reversed continuous, "grey" NA. Breaking: removed pal_* functions. Removed unnecessary messages. Fixed bug col_breaks working numeric col. Fixed bug str_keep_seq working date, datetime time class. Fixed bug time class variables work. Fixed bug gg_contour_filled gg_density_filled default number colours. Improved default positional breaks. Ensured positional breaks take expand account. Updated gg_qq, includes geom_qq_line layer, nicer x_title, y_title coord defaults. Added positional scale arguments gg_sf (.e. *_limits, *_expand, *_breaks, *_labels). Improved margins col_legend_place top.

title: ggblanket 5.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2023-09-19 Fixed bug col variable reversing non-flipped. Added pal_light_mode pal_dark_mode.

title: ggblanket 5.0.0
text: CRAN release: 2023-09-12 Added mapping argument allow extra aesthetics included, shape (use delayed evaluation aesthetics col, colour, fill alpha). Added stat argument back flexibility. Added linetype_title, shape_title, size_title arguments. Added str_keep_seq helper function support keeping labels sequence. Breaking: made horizontal y characters factors plot values low bottom high top. Breaking: removed lower, middle, upper, xlower, xmiddle, xupper arguments gg_blank. Updated *_breaks defaults. Made plot order logical variables TRUE first. Added pal_discrete2 6 colour blind safe colours light background theme.

title: ggblanket 4.0.0
text: CRAN release: 2023-08-08 Breaking: removed stat argument gg_* functions except gg_blank. Breaking: renamed pal_hue pal_discrete made colours relatively red/green colour blind safe. Breaking: renamed gg_bin2d gg_bin_2d. Breaking: changed *_oob default back scales::oob_keep. Improved scales work better transformations, limits, breaks etc. Added gg_contour, gg_contour_filled, gg_density2d, gg_density2d_filled functions. Made default keep unused factors across scales facets. Made default continuous label function drop trailing zeros. Made default legend place simpler: bottom, unless continuous. Otherwise right. Added text aesthetic plotly::ggplotly.

title: ggblanket 3.0.0
text: CRAN release: 2023-06-30 New theme functions: light_mode dark_mode. Breaking: removed gg_theme. Breaking: renamed *_grid arguments *_gridlines. Breaking: removed void argument. Breaking: removed gg_function. Breaking: made col_rescale require function (e.g. scales::rescale()). Added facet_switch argument. Made col_legend_place = "none" remove col legend. Made gg_bin2d gg_hex scales calculate way functions. raster, removed col aesthetic legend work. Improved gg_sf default alpha. Supported gg_sf work non-standard named geometry. Thanks Nik Mitchell Rosie Percival feedback.

title: ggblanket 2.0.0
text: CRAN release: 2023-05-26 Made ggblanket work better adding layers. Made pal longer inherit layers col aesthetic. Made x y scales work better, including NA limits. Added 2 colours pal_hue. Breaking: changed default *oob argument scales::oob_censor. Breaking: changed default coord argument include clip = "" argument. Breaking: removed add_tooltip function. Breaking: changed pal functions pal_blue, pal_grey, pal_hue.

title: ggblanket 1.8.0
text: CRAN release: 2023-04-24 Updated default colour palette. Updated default NA colour.

title: ggblanket 1.7.0
text: CRAN release: 2023-03-06 Updated gg_theme default axis-line gridline thickness. Supported named pal vectors. Removed clip argument. Added coord argument. Added *_oob arguments. Improved *_limits works. Removed default gridlines gg_sf. Fixed bug default x_expand. Fixed bug gg_histogram scales. Critical fix support dplyr 1.1.0. Supported colouring date, datetime time variables. Made datetime breaks default waiver. Breaking: removed void argument gg_theme. Corrected bug gg_raster legend showing. Updated gg_raster *_limits *_expand defaults. Internal function make infinite values NA made compatible dplyr 1.1.0 (#269, @DavisVaughan)

title: ggblanket 1.6.0
text: CRAN release: 2022-11-18 Breaking: renamed add_tooltip_text add_tooltip changed column name “tooltip”. Breaking: coord argument removed. Added clip argument defaulted “”. Made x y limits also act within coord. Made gg_blank powerful flexible. Improved horizontal flipped plot scales. Fixed bugs relating free facet_scales scale limits. Improved vignette.

title: ggblanket 1.5.0
text: CRAN release: 2022-10-14 Breaking: redesigned gg_theme. Added automatic gridline removal. Added gg_bin2d function. Added gg_hex function. Added col_trans col_rescale arguments. Made col_legend_place default “r” numeric col variable. Made *_title = "" equivalent + labs(* = NULL). Supported x y datetime variables. Supported x y time variables. Redesigned internal code x, y col scales.

title: ggblanket 1.4.0
text: CRAN release: 2022-09-02 Breaking: Removed col_intervals argument. Breaking: Removed *_oob argument. Breaking: gg_theme, changed *_style arguments *_face. Breaking: gg_theme, changed font arguments family. Added gg_polygon function. Added col_continuous argument support colouring “steps”. Added col_legend_rev argument reverse legends. Added facet_layout argument faceting flexibility. Added facet_space argument support proportional facet panels. Removed width default. Improved default legend look continuous gradient. Improved scales y NULL plot horizontal. Fixed bug position = "fill". Updated messages. Added vignette.

title: ggblanket 1.3.0
text: CRAN release: 2022-08-05 Breaking: removed facet_intervals argument. Breaking: made extensive changes gg_theme function. Added facet2 aesthetic support effortless grid faceting. Changed default background theme colours. Fixed bug *_limits *_include working correctly. Added *_sec_axis arguments. Changed *_oob default scales::oob_keep. Removed reversal logical variable order. Defaulted col_legend_place "b".

title: ggblanket 1.2.0
text: CRAN release: 2022-07-01 Breaking: Removed *_breaks_n *_breaks_width. Added support *_breaks receive function. Added *_include argument. Added *_trans argument. Modified default x_breaks behaviour x y numeric date. Enhanced gg_blank work one x y. Provided support ggplotly. Added new add_tooltip_text function. Added new titles argument apply function across titles. Support gg_ functions working stats. Removed size argument, anticipation ggplot2 3.4.0. Updated gg_theme title vjust margins.

title: ggblanket 1.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2022-05-27 Remove default sentence case transformation categorical variable labels. Updated scales work consistent way position = "fill".

title: ggblanket 1.0.0
text: CRAN release: 2022-05-10 Initial release.
